title: AI for Accessibility

modules.title: Assistive Technology Applications
modules.sub: Exploring AI-powered tools that empower people with disabilities
modules.description: Learners survey the landscape of AI-driven assistive technologies, from screen readers and speech recognition to real-time captioning and navigation aids. They evaluate real-world applications across visual, auditory, motor, and cognitive disabilities, identifying which tools address which needs. By the end, learners can match specific assistive AI solutions to particular accessibility challenges.

modules.title: Inclusive AI Design
modules.sub: Building AI systems that work for every user from the start
modules.description: Learners examine the principles and practices that make AI systems inclusive by design rather than as an afterthought. They analyze case studies where poor design choices created barriers, then apply inclusive design frameworks to critique and improve sample AI interfaces. The module challenges learners to draft a set of design requirements for an AI product that proactively serves users with diverse abilities.

modules.title: Breaking Down Barriers
modules.sub: Identifying and dismantling systemic obstacles in AI access
modules.description: Learners investigate the technical, organizational, and societal barriers that prevent people with disabilities from benefiting equally from AI technologies. They assess issues such as biased training data, inaccessible interfaces, and policy gaps through guided case analysis. Learners then propose targeted interventions to reduce or eliminate specific barriers within a chosen AI context.

modules.title: Universal Access Principles
modules.sub: Applying enduring standards to ensure AI reaches everyone
modules.description: Learners engage with established universal design standards—such as WCAG and the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities—and map them onto AI product development lifecycles. They work through a structured audit exercise, evaluating an AI system against these principles and documenting gaps and recommendations. The module culminates in learners articulating a personal or organizational commitment to embedding universal access into future AI work.

synopsis: Discover how AI technologies can help people with disabilities access information and services. Learn principles for designing inclusive AI systems.
